message:
refactor(sqlite): gate FTS consistency repair behind #[cfg(test)] and update indexer to use timeout opens

Follows up on the FTS deferral refactor to fully isolate the legacy FTS
consistency repair machinery from production code paths, and updates the
indexer to use timeout-aware storage opens for better multi-process safety.

Production code changes:
- Gate ensure_search_fallback_fts_consistency(), ensure_fts_consistency_via_
  frankensqlite(), read_fts_franken_rebuild_generation(), and
  record_fts_franken_rebuild_generation() behind #[cfg(test)] since these
  are no longer called from any production path — only from test assertions
- Gate the FTS_FRANKEN_REBUILD_META_KEY and FTS_FRANKEN_REBUILD_GENERATION
  constants behind #[cfg(test)] as well
- Replace all FrankenStorage::open() calls in the indexer with
  open_franken_storage_with_timeout() using a 10-second timeout to prevent
  indexer hangs when another process holds the database lock

Search client changes:
- Remove unused FrankenStorage import (now only uses open helpers)
- Add #[cfg(test)] import for OptionalExtension used only in test assertions

E2E test updates:
- Rename test from "duplicate_fts_schema_rows_are_repaired" to
  "duplicate_fts_schema_rows_do_not_block_cli_reads_and_writes" reflecting
  the new design where Tantivy is authoritative and malformed FTS is tolerated
- Replace run_sqlite3/python3 fixture helper with direct rusqlite calls for
  schema corruption injection — more reliable, no external process dependency
- Add explicit search-after-injection assertion proving Tantivy continues to
  return results even when SQLite FTS is broken
- Remove post-repair assertions that checked FTS table row counts and SQLite
  client readability since the FTS table is no longer eagerly repaired
- Add materialize_fresh_fts_schema_via_rusqlite call in unit test setup to
  ensure the canonical FTS schema exists before duplicate injection
- Update all assertion messages to reflect the "tolerate malformed FTS"
  design rather than the old "repair FTS on every open" design

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
